characters.name: Mira
characters.description: woman in her twenties, dark hair tied back, patched red wool cloak, heavy canvas pack, dented brass lantern hanging from the pack

scenes.title: Leaving the cottage at dawn
scenes.source_excerpt: Mira laced her boots in the grey light before dawn and stepped out of the cottage. She shouldered her pack, heavy with bread and a single brass lantern, and did not look back at the door she might never open again.
scenes.prompt: A cinematic shot opens tight on a young woman's hands cinching her worn leather boot-laces, then cranes up with her as she rises and steps through a low doorway straight toward the lens. The camera eases back to let her fill the frame, hitching a heavy canvas pack higher as she passes — dark hair tied back, a patched red wool cloak, a dented brass lantern swinging at her side. It settles into a slow push-in as she stops on the threshold, one hand on the door frame, and turns to camera to say, barely a whisper, "Goodbye. I'll come back for you." Behind her, shuttered stone cottages recede down an ash-grey pre-dawn lane in flat cool-blue twilight with no hard shadow, as [a thin wind moans over empty rooftops, her boots creak on the frost, a lone dog barks far off].
scenes.negative_prompt: text overlay, subtitles, warped hands, extra people, bright sunlight, static frozen frame

dialogue.speaker: Mira
dialogue.line: Goodbye. I'll come back for you.

scenes.title: A vow at the wheat fields
scenes.source_excerpt: At the edge of the wheat fields she paused. A wind moved through the stalks, bending them all one way, and somewhere a lone crow called. "I will come back," she said to no one, and the words fell flat in the empty air.
scenes.prompt: A wide establishing shot holds the young woman small at the edge of a vast wheat field, her back to the lens, then slowly pushes in as she turns her face toward camera, jaw tight. The camera keeps closing until her eyes fill the frame in a tight close-up, her patched red cloak and loose dark hair streaming across the shot, the brass lantern swinging into and out of frame at her hip. Around her the tall golden wheat bends all one way in a hard gust and a lone black crow cuts up through the top of the frame into a pale ash sky. Holding the close-up, she says in a quiet, unwavering voice, "I will come back. I swear it." Cool overcast dawn light, muted gold and grey, as [dry wheat hisses in the wind, her cloak snaps and cracks, a single harsh crow caw lands as she finishes].

dialogue.speaker: Mira
dialogue.line: I will come back. I swear it.
